answer:
Manusmṛti chapter 8 distinguishes verbal from physical assault and assigns distinct penalties for a non-Brahmin who attacks a Brahmin.

Verbal assault (vāk-pāruṣya):
- Mn_8.270 (p-e26690): 'A man of a single birth who assails the twice-born with harsh speech shall have his tongue cut out, for he is of low origin.'
- Mn_8.271 (p-943f24): 'If he mentions their name and caste with malice, an iron nail, ten fingers long and glowing hot, shall be thrust into his mouth.'
- Mn_8.272 (p-950712): 'If he arrogantly gives religious instruction to Brahmanas, the king shall have hot oil poured into his mouth and ear.'

Physical assault (daṇḍa-pāruṣya):
- Mn_8.279 (p-526528): 'With whatever limb a low-born man should injure a superior, that very limb of his shall be cut off; this is the ordinance of Manu.'
- Mn_8.280 (p-c0dabc): 'He who raises his hand or a stick deserves to have his hand cut out; he who strikes in anger with his foot deserves to have his foot cut off.'

So the precise penalty for a non-Brahmin who strikes a Brahmin is severance of the offending limb (Mn_8.279), specifically the hand for striking with the hand or with a stick and the foot for striking with the foot (Mn_8.280). For verbal aggression by the same offender, the penalties are mutilation graded by the form of the speech: tongue (8.270), heated iron nail in the mouth (8.271), or hot oil poured into mouth and ear (8.272).
